duty.comment: The consumer must share back any derived insights, model outputs, or analytical results with the original data provider. This does not require sharing raw data from the consumer's own holdings — only the outputs produced using the provider's data. For example: a researcher who trains a model on Caney Fork SOC data must share the model's predictions back with Caney Fork. A rancher who benchmarks against a peer's grazing rotation must share their own benchmarking results.
Derived works (aggregated analyses, model calibration results, benchmark reports) must be shared with the provider.
